query: Calculate a list of duplicate md5 sum hashes for all the ".java" files in the /testbed directory
command_text: find /testbed -type f -name '*.java' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | uniq -w32 -d -c | awk '{print $1, $2}'

query: Calculate md5 sum of the md5 sum of all the sorted files under /testbed/dir2/subdir2
gold: find /testbed/dir2/subdir2 -type f -print0 | sort -z | xargs -r0 md5sum | md5sum
command_text: find /testbed/dir2/subdir2 -type f -print0 | sort -z | xargs -0 md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | tr -d '\n' | md5sum

query: Calculate the md5 sum of the sorted list of md5 sums of all ".py" files under /testbed/dir1/subdir1
gold: find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name *.py -exec md5sum {} + | awk '{print $1}' | sort | md5sum
command_text: find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort -k1,1 | awk '{print $1}' | tr '\n' ' ' | md5sum

query: Calculate the md5sum of each ".py" file under /testbed/dir1/subdir1, sort the output, and calculate the md5sum of that
gold: find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name "*.py" -exec md5sum {} + | awk '{print $1}' | sort | md5sum
command_text: find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | md5sum

query: Calculate the total disk usage for each ".txt" file on the /testbed directory and prepend the system host name to the output
command_text: find /testbed -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.txt' -exec du -b {} + | awk -v h="$(hostname)" '{print h": "$1" "$2}'
agent_observation: $1: -c: line 1: unexpected EOF while looking for matching `''

query: Check if current shell is running within a 'screen' process.

command_text: if [ "$(grep -F -x -f /testbed/dir3/subdir1/subsubdir1/textfile3.txt /testbed/dir2/subdir1/textfile2.txt | wc -l)" -eq "$(wc -l < /testbed/dir3/subdir1/subsubdir1/textfile3.txt)" ]; then echo "subset"; else echo "not subset"; fi

query: Compute the mean average of the word count of *.txt files in the /testbed directory
Compute the mean average of the word count of *.txt files smaller than 2000 words in the /testbed directory
gold: find /testbed  -name '*.txt' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -w | awk '$1 < 2000 {v += $1; c++} END {print v/c}'
command_text: find /testbed -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.txt' -exec wc -w {} + | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1;cnt++} END{print sum/cnt}'

Create a table containing all information from /testbed/dir1/subdir1/textfile4.txt and /testbed/dir2/subdir2/textfile5.txt, merging lines where the first field of both files matches, and keeping the line that starts with "Gene" at the start of the file.
gold: join -a1 -a2 <(sed s/^Gene/00ne/ /testbed/dir1/subdir1/textfile4.txt | sort) <(sed s/^Gene/00ne/ /testbed/dir2/subdir2/textfile5.txt | sort) | column -t | sed s/^00ne/Gene/
command_text: (echo Gene; awk 'NR==FNR{a[$1]=$0;next} $1 in a{print a[$1] FS $0; delete a[$1]} END{for(i in a)print a[i]}' /testbed/dir1/subdir1/textfile4.txt /testbed/dir2/subdir2/textfile5.txt)
